signals.headline: Key developments:**
signals.raw:
Key developments:**

South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026, exceeding the central bank's 2% target and prompting the Bank of Korea to raise its full-year inflation outlook to 2.2% from 2.1%.[1] The escalating Middle East conflict has become a critical driver, as surging oil costs are pushing consumer prices higher.[3] South Korea is particularly vulnerable to energy shocks give

Bank of Korea Holds Rate at 2.5% Amid Rising Oil-Driven Inflation | NO | HIGH confidence**

The Bank of Korea maintained its benchmark interest rate at 2.5% for the seventh consecutive month following its Monetary Policy Board decision on Friday, April 10, 2026.[3] While this decision was widely anticipated, it reflects the central bank's concern about inflationary pressures rather than a shift in
